verse_specific_anchors: amṛtatvāya kalpate ('is fit for the imperishable') preserved as 'fit for the imperishable kind of work — long-horizon'
sama-duḥkha-sukham preserved as 'equal-minded toward pain and pleasure' — categorical equivalence, not phenomenal identity
the qualifier-not-outcome structure: 'kalpate, fitness, not personality' — direct rendering of the verse's precondition framing
yam hi na vyathayanty ete ('whom these do not disturb') — preserved as 'whom these sensor-contacts do not disturb'
verdict_reason: Tested against three random verses from Chapters 6, 11, and 16: BG 6.34 (cañcalaṁ hi manaḥ — the mind is restless), BG 11.41 (sakheti matvā — having considered you a friend), BG 16.21 (tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ — the threefold gate of hell). The 2.15 engineering analog (qualifier for long-horizon work via trained equanimity) cannot equally apply to any of these. The kalpate-as-qualifier structure is uniquely a 2.15 reading; it depends on the precondition framing the test verses lack. PASS.
